description_long: This object is a roughly triangular, elongated stone with a pointed tip and a broad, flat base. The surface is marked by natural striations and subtle color variations, ranging from light gray to brownish hues. One side features a visible inscription or marking, suggesting it may have been cataloged or studied. The edges are irregular and slightly chipped, giving it a rugged, unpolished appearance. The stone is relatively thin when viewed from the side, with a consistent taper from base to tip. Its overall form and texture suggest it could serve as a primitive tool, marker, or decorative element in an urban landscape.
description: A pointed, triangular stone with rough, striated surfaces and natural color variations, featuring an inscription on one side.
description_view_0: The broad, flat face of the stone is visible, showing natural striations and a pointed tip.
description_view_1: A thin, edge-on profile reveals the stone's narrow width and elongated taper.
description_view_2: The opposite broad face is shown, featuring a visible inscription and similar surface texture.
description_view_3: Another edge-on view, highlighting the stone's thinness and irregular side contours.
attribute_placement: set as a standalone feature or marker on the ground
attribute_pattern: Natural striations and subtle veining run vertically along the surface, with irregular, organic patterns and a visible inscription on one side.
attribute_weathering: The stone appears rough and unpolished, with minor chipping and surface irregularities indicating moderate weathering but no significant erosion.
attribute_integration_with_surroundings: Typically contrasts with manicured lawns or paved surfaces, but may blend with other natural stones or landscaping elements in a park or garden setting.
